data.content: I can see the problem. The target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't know about it. I'll also add it to `FileWrites` so `Clean` works properly.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but the compiler doesn't know about it. The target needs to add the generated file to `<Compile>` and also to `<FileWrites>` for clean support.

Let me fix this by adding the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never told the compiler about it. Glob-based `<Compile>` items are evaluated at *evaluation time* (before any targets run), so files created during the build aren't automatically picked up.

**Fix:** Added two lines inside the target's `<ItemGroup>`:
- `<Compile Include="...">` — adds the generated file to the compilation
- `<FileWrites Include="...">` — registers it so `dotnet clean` removes it
